column1: # lockstep-manifest.tsv — contract pairs enforced by scripts/check-lockstep-pairs.sh.
# TAB-separated. Columns:
#   pair-id <TAB> relation <TAB> fileA <TAB> anchorA <TAB> fileB <TAB> anchorB
# relation: verbatim | subset-of   (see the script header for exact semantics)
# Paths are repo-root-relative. Blocks are delimited in-file by LOCKSTEP-BEGIN/END <anchor>.
# What belongs here: a contract duplicated across files where the ONLY thing keeping the
# copies together is a prose instruction. What does NOT: pairs already mechanically
# enforced elsewhere — model tiers (check-model-tiers.sh), the reviewer registry, the
# section catalog, statectl's codegen byte-match (statectl-selftest drift-check), the
# text-contract carriers, config-lint vs schema. Duplicate machinery is worse than none.
# Decision-Ledger provenance enum. ledger-lint.sh holds the canonical literal; plan-lint's
# HUMAN_PROVENANCE is a deliberate narrowing to the human-attributed values, so this is
# subset-of and NOT verbatim. The prose canonical (interviewing-baseline) and the agent-md
# mirrors are a fourth and fifth representation in bullet/prose form — not byte-anchorable
# without authoring new canonical literals, so they stay reviewer-guarded.
provenance-enum
# AC-ID positional fallback rule. Both docs already SAY they must match byte-for-byte;
# nothing checked it until now. The markers sit after each site's differing parenthetical
# prefix, so only the shared rule body is compared — the prefixes stay free to differ.
ac-id-rule
# FINDINGS_SCHEMA triple. The Workflow runtime gives these scripts no import, so each
# declares its own copy; stall-probe and tool-discipline-probe exist to reproduce
# production behavior exactly, which is worthless if their schema has drifted. A triple is
# expressed as two pairs against the same canonical leg (code-review.mjs). design-sync.mjs
# is NOT a leg — it only references the schema in a comment.
findings-schema-stall
findings-schema-tdp
# Stage-7/8 dual-target branches: the selftests embed a mirror of the stage doc's bash and
# test THAT, so a doc-side edit the mirror does not track means the selftest is green about
# a behavior production no longer has. These replace the deleted (B) prose-presence halves.
stage7-dual-target
stage8-secondary-review
# DROPPED to reviewer-guarded — recorded here rather than forgotten. Each was considered
# for the mechanical manifest and rejected for a stated reason.
# preflight ↔ verifyctl zero-verifying-lane predicate. preflight.sh computes an aggregated
# VERIFYING count inline; verifyctl.sh reads allowUnverified / lanes / extraLanes into
# separate variables under a different jq arg name. No byte-identical block exists, and
# reaching one means restructuring working code for the benefit of its own guard. The
# intent is already declared in prose at preflight.sh's VERIFYING comment ("Keep this set
# in lockstep with verifyctl.sh").
# Stage-8 secondary-review loop, past the clean-worktree assertion. The stage doc
# interleaves in-session review prose that the selftest's bash mirror deliberately omits,
# so no single contiguous block spans the rest of the loop. The pinned span above covers
# the preamble and the clean-worktree assertion; the remainder is reviewer-guarded.
# GH_BOT config-dir basename (install-gh-bot.sh ↔ claim-issue.sh). Semantically parallel,
# not byte-parallel — forcing parity means refactoring working code for its own guard.
# intake-pin- worktree-name coupling (stages/1-intake.md Step 1.P ↔ stages/10-cleanup.md).
# The Stage-1 read pin is created under an `intake-pin-<issue>` name and removed by the
# Stage-10 backstop; a ONE-SIDED rename orphans the cleanup. Considered for a row and
# DROPPED: the two sites carry no byte-identical anchorable line without restructuring
# working prose, the two greps that used to assert it (intake-readroot-selftest.sh checks
# 5-6, deleted in #214) were independent presence checks — a consistent rename false-passed
# both — and the missed regression is a benign per-issue-namespaced detached worktree leak
# on crashed runs, not a verdict path. Reviewer-guarded.
